sections.heading: About
sections.content: AltsPassport is an AI-powered market network that connects alternative fund managers with investors. They had a proof of concept product and a website, but both fell short of the founders' vision — investors struggled to understand their product, and visuals were underdeveloped. With investor conversations on the horizon, they needed a significant lift across their product and web presence. Pepperplane brought in UX strategy support, and I led the visual direction, website design and high-fidelity UI.

sections.heading: Process
sections.content: We started at the foundation — workshopping their brand, business proposition and target customers to establish a clear strategic direction for the website's content and visuals. From there I developed an updated visual language that enhanced their existing brand and respected their wish to keep their existing logo: Sharper, more distinctive, and deliberately different from the blue and white that is the go-to in their competitive landscape. The UX work ran in parallel, and I used Figma and Figma Make to translate user interviews, problem statements, content from ideation workshops, and user journeys into interactive prototypes and high-fidelity product UI

sections.heading: Outcome
sections.content: AltsPassport received a new website, a distinctive visual identity, and polished product UI and collateral ready for investor and sales conversations. Their refreshed brand gives them a differentiated presence that supports their unique vision. The combination of UX and UI work helped bring their full vision together into something cohesive and credible.
